hypothesis: Training LocalRetro and MEGAN with an inverse-template-frequency class-balanced loss will increase top-10 accuracy on USPTO-retro test reactions from templates occurring ≤10 times by at least 15 percentage points while reducing accuracy on templates occurring ≥10,001 times by no more than 2 percentage points, and will raise top-10 accuracy on Pistachio OOD by at least 0.5 percentage points, relative to unweighted training on the same splits.
